summary: Skill Challenges are optional party-facing structured scenes resolved by multiple skill checks, usually tracking a target number of successes before a smaller number of failures. They are encounter-like workflows, not static actor math.

integrationPoints.skillRolls: Reuse existing actor skill roll/skill math. The challenge app should consume finalized roll totals instead of recalculating skills.
integrationPoints.chatCards: Emit progress cards after each accepted check, with GM controls for accept, reject, convert to aid another, or manually adjust successes/failures.
integrationPoints.feats: Skill Challenge feats stay metadata-only until the app exists; then the app can read actor feat slugs and expose eligible buttons/reroll/effect toggles.
integrationPoints.combat: Allow use during combat, but keep state in the challenge engine, not in CombatEngine. Combat actions can reference a running challenge by id.
integrationPoints.gmDatapad: Optional launcher/monitor panel after MVP. Do not make GM Datapad the source of truth.

phasePlan.title: Metadata and audit only
phasePlan.deliverable: Current Phase 3 manifest and design reference.

phasePlan.title: Engine and JSON model
phasePlan.deliverable: Pure state transition functions plus tests for successes, failures, complexity, effects, and manual overrides.

phasePlan.title: GM app and chat card
phasePlan.deliverable: Create/edit/run a Skill Challenge; accept skill rolls; emit progress chat cards.

phasePlan.title: Feat hooks
phasePlan.deliverable: Skill Challenge: Recovery, Last Resort, and Catastrophic Avoidance appear as app-level options when a participant has the feat.
